items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a development project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled and rescheduled for an unspecified date. The project will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is confirmed, meaning neighbors should watch for future announcements about this development.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant
items.summary: The council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7th. This project addresses traffic safety at the intersection of a major avenue and railroad crossing.

items.headline: City awards contract for 2026 street pavement repairs
items.summary: San José awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project on April 7th. The project will resurface multiple local streets across the city.

items.headline: City updating Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is revising its Active Transportation Plan (Phase 2), including changes to how projects are evaluated and prioritized. This plan affects biking, walking, and transit infrastructure across the city.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract, though the agenda does not specify which city infrastructure or public facility is being built. Residents may want to check the full meeting minutes to learn what improvements are coming to Milpitas.

items.headline: Milpitas updates federal housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council approved a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 budget related to federal housing and community development funds. This affects how the city allocates money for affordable housing and community programs over the next several years.

items.headline: Major senior housing complex approved in San José
items.summary: Trillium senior apartments will add 65 affordable units at 678 E St John St with nearly $8.8M in investment. The project addresses San José's acute shortage of senior housing options.

items.headline: San José adopts emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: The City Council approved a new Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy on April 1st. This policy will affect where temporary housing for unhoused residents can be located throughout San José.
